name: Gabled Roof (Tejado a dos aguas)
description: A two-pitch (a dos aguas) roof: two symmetric sloped faces meeting at a ridge along the long axis of the footprint. Sits ON TOP of the given AABB and rises floor(min(W, D)/2) levels to the gable peak. Pitched faces use stair blocks tilted inward; the two short ends are filled with a triangular vertical wall (gable). Suitable for houses, halls, gatehouses, and any rectangular base where shedding rain is desired.

required_furniture.rule: Solid course along the ridge crest (1 wide when min(W,D) is odd, 2 wide when even).
Perimeter of every pitched layer; each stair faces inward toward the ridge so its slope rises toward the peak.
Vertical triangular infill at the two short ends (the gable walls); uses @accent or @primary.

placement_rules: Pitched faces use stair blocks facing inward (toward the ridge).
Gable ends are vertical infill (a flat triangular wall), not pitched.
Ridge runs along the long axis of the footprint by default; can be overridden via the ridge_axis kwarg ('x' or 'z').
Roof peak height equals floor(min(W, D) / 2) — the roof EXTENDS BEYOND the AABB.y1 top.
The base layer of the roof sits at y = aabb.y1 (immediately above the top of the wall AABB).
lighting_ventilation: The attic volume between the two pitched faces is left hollow (air); skylights or dormer windows can be added by a follow-up skill.

medieval.notes: Steep pitch, dark wood shingles over stone gables.

modern.notes: Shallow pitch favored; consider scaling peak down externally.

fantasy.notes: Tall, dramatic pitch; gable end is a focal point for ornament.
